tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts.
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BRMHMIKSA7LRPAY6FER522K6CQVUU6XSW/01JGHJEH5XQ0SJ9EZ4VF8B5Y94</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.assistant.chat</typename>
</object>

Enable the planning skill (key: org.dxos.skill.planning) using the skill manager.

Create exactly 3 plan tasks with update-tasks for writing a short haiku (3 lines) on these topics:
1. spring rain
2. ocean waves
3. night stars

Work through the tasks one at a time:
- Mark only the current task in-progress.
- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

Completion criteria:
- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.
- A 3-line haiku was written for each topic (spring rain, ocean waves, night stars).
<input>{}</input>

  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.

Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

<output_format>

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">

    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">

    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true

    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts.
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BRMHMIKSA7LRPAY6FER522K6CQVUU6XSW/01JGHJEH5XQ0SJ9EZ4VF8B5Y94</dxn>

</object>

Enable the planning skill (key: org.dxos.skill.planning) using the skill manager.

Create exactly 3 plan tasks with update-tasks for writing a short haiku (3 lines) on these topics:
1. spring rain
2. ocean waves
3. night stars

Work through the tasks one at a time:
- Mark only the current task in-progress.
- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

Completion criteria:
- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.
- A 3-line haiku was written for each topic (spring rain, ocean waves, night stars).

The error isn't about permissions though—it's a service availability issue. The AiContextService appears to be unavailable, which is preventing the enable operation from completing.

content.text:
The `enable-skills` tool is failing with a service not available error. The planning skill doesn't have `agentCanEnable: true` in its properties - looking at the skill list, only skills with `"agentCanEnable": true` can be enabled by the agent. The planning skill (`org.dxos.skill.planning`) does not have this flag set.

However, the error is actually a service availability error, not a permissions error. Let me check if the planning skill's tools are already available to me. Looking at the tools I have, I don't see `update-tasks` or similar planning tools.

Wait, the error message says "ServiceNotAvailable" which is a system-level error, not a permission issue. 

The `enable-skills` function itself is working, but the underlying service isn't available. Since the planning skill lacks the `agentCanEnable: true` flag anyway, I can't enable it regardless - only Agent Wizard, Browser, Database, Inbox, Memory, and Web Search have that capability.

The error message points to a system-level issue with the AiContextService rather than a permissions problem. Without access to the `update-tasks` tool and facing this service unavailability, I need to report this as a failure.

The `enable-skills` tool is consistently failing with a `ServiceNotAvailable` error. I cannot enable the planning skill, which means I cannot access the `update-tasks` tool needed to create and manage plan tasks. I must report this failure honestly.

failure.message: Unable to enable the planning skill. The enable-skills tool fails with: ServiceNotAvailable: Service not available: @dxos/assistant/AiContextService. Without the planning skill enabled, the update-tasks tool is not available, so plan tasks cannot be created or managed.
failure.description: Attempted to enable the planning skill (org.dxos.skill.planning) three times using the enable-skills tool, but each attempt returned the same error: 'ServiceNotAvailable: Service not available: @dxos/assistant/AiContextService (affinity=process) — no LayerSpec contributes this service — is the providing plugin activated on SetupProcessManager?'. This is a system-level service availability issue that prevents skill enablement. Additionally, the planning skill does not have agentCanEnable=true in its metadata, which may also be a contributing factor.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

1. spring rain
2. ocean waves

Work through the tasks one at a time:

- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

Creates a new object and adds it to the current space.
Get the schema from the schema-list tool and ensure that the data matches the corresponding schema.
References are provided in the following format: { "/": "echo:..." }.
Reference examples: { "/": "echo:/01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }, { "/": "echo://<space id>/01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4" }

tools.name: delete-object
tools.description: Deletes the object.

obj.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

obj.description:

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.

Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.

Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.

  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]

  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]

  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.

</skill>

  <dxn>echo://BXJIVMWIYSO4P24VCMHP44G5UAUNRV5A5/01JGHJEH5XQ0SJ9EZ4VF8B5Y94</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.assistant.chat</typename>
</object>

Enable the planning skill (key: org.dxos.skill.planning) using the skill manager.

Create exactly 3 plan tasks with update-tasks for writing a short haiku (3 lines) on these topics:
1. spring rain
2. ocean waves
3. night stars

Work through the tasks one at a time:
- Mark only the current task in-progress.
- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

Completion criteria:
- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.
- A 3-line haiku was written for each topic (spring rain, ocean waves, night stars).
<input>{}</input>

Let me use the update-tasks tool to create the tasks. But I need to check
what tools are available now. The planning skill provides "org
.dxos.function.planning.updateTasks" tool. Let me look for the update-tasks tool... I don
't see it in my available tools list. Let me check if there's a tool called "update-tasks"

or similar.

back at my available tools, I'm not finding the updateTasks function

task objects, or see if the skill somehow made the
tool available in a way I'm not recognizing.
'm wondering if the update-tasks tool becomes available once the skill
is enabled, even though it's not in my current functions list. Let me try checking the schemas to see if there's a task type I
can work with directly, or I could attempt to call the tool since
the system instructions mention it should be available.
The Planning skill is enabled. Now let me check
available schemas for tasks and create the plan tasks.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BXJIVMWIYSO4P24VCMHP44G5UAUNRV5A5/01JGHJEH5XQ0SJ9EZ4VF8B5Y94</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.assistant.chat</typename>
</object>

Enable the planning skill (key: org.dxos.skill.planning) using the skill manager.

Create exactly 3 plan tasks with update-tasks for writing a short haiku (3 lines) on these topics:
1. spring rain
2. ocean waves
3. night stars

Work through the tasks one at a time:
- Mark only the current task in-progress.
- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

Completion criteria:
- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.
- A 3-line haiku was written for each topic (spring rain, ocean waves, night stars).
<input>{}</input>

tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

    "text": "cyberdyne bob",

</example>

    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3

</example>

    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>

Enable the planning skill (key: org.dxos.skill.planning) using the skill manager.

Create exactly 3 plan tasks with update-tasks for writing a short haiku (3 lines) on these topics:
1. spring rain
2. ocean waves
3. night stars

Work through the tasks one at a time:
- Mark only the current task in-progress.
- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

Completion criteria:
- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.
- A 3-line haiku was written for each topic (spring rain, ocean waves, night stars).

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

Enable the planning skill (key: org.dxos.skill.planning) using the skill manager.

Create exactly 3 plan tasks with update-tasks for writing a short haiku (3 lines) on these topics:
1. spring rain
2. ocean waves
3. night stars

Work through the tasks one at a time:
- Mark only the current task in-progress.
- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

Completion criteria:
- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.
- A 3-line haiku was written for each topic (spring rain, ocean waves, night stars).

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  
</skill>

## Context Objects

  <dxn>echo://BUFY7JEWAWEL6LY54H3Q2ZREBYMODWB4S/01JGHJEH5XQ0SJ9EZ4VF8B5Y94</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.assistant.chat</typename>
</object>

Enable the planning skill (key: org.dxos.skill.planning) using the skill manager.

Create exactly 3 plan tasks with update-tasks for writing a short haiku (3 lines) on these topics:
1. spring rain
2. ocean waves
3. night stars

Work through the tasks one at a time:
- Mark only the current task in-progress.
- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

Completion criteria:
- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.
- A 3-line haiku was written for each topic (spring rain, ocean waves, night stars).
<input>{}</input>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">

    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3

</example>

    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

Create exactly 3 plan tasks with update-tasks for writing a short haiku (3 lines) on these topics:
1. spring rain

Work through the tasks one at a time:
- Mark only the current task in-progress.
- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

Completion criteria:
- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.
- A 3-line haiku was written for each topic (spring rain, ocean waves, night stars).

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.

Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

<skill>

Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3

</example>

    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

</skill>

Enable the planning skill (key: org.dxos.skill.planning) using the skill manager.

Create exactly 3 plan tasks with update-tasks for writing a short haiku (3 lines) on these topics:
1. spring rain
2. ocean waves
3. night stars

Work through the tasks one at a time:
- Mark only the current task in-progress.
- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

Completion criteria:
- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.
- A 3-line haiku was written for each topic (spring rain, ocean waves, night stars).
<input>{}</input>

tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

    "text": "cyberdyne bob",

</example>

    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true

    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

</object>

Enable the planning skill (key: org.dxos.skill.planning) using the skill manager.

Create exactly 3 plan tasks with update-tasks for writing a short haiku (3 lines) on these topics:
1. spring rain
2. ocean waves
3. night stars

Work through the tasks one at a time:
- Mark only the current task in-progress.
- Write the haiku for that topic in your response (visible in the chat feed).
- Mark that task done with update-tasks before starting the next task.

When all three haikus are written and all tasks are done, call completeJob.

Completion criteria:
- Planning skill is enabled successfully.
- Exactly 3 tasks exist in the plan for the three haiku topics.
- All 3 tasks are marked done.
- A 3-line haiku was written for each topic (spring rain, ocean waves, night stars).
- Agent used "update-tasks" tool and did not manupulate the objects directly.

Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.

  }

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.

Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.
